_comment: Games whose real packaging is NOT a standard rectangular cardboard box. From a vision audit of every cover (scripts/gallery-cache/_shapes) plus product-knowledge additions where the flat cover art hid the true shape. `source`: 'image' = visible in the cover, 'product-knowledge' = known real packaging not visible in art. Consumed by future rendering work — see PACKAGE-SHAPES.md. Absence of an entry ⇒ treated as a box.

dobble-63268.note: Circular metal tin, visible cylindrical body + lid rim. capCrop isolates the round lid art from the angled product shot.

chupacabra-survive-the-night-120515.note: Cylindrical canister/dice-cup tube, curved body + rounded top. Cover art wraps the side.

love-letter-277085.note: Plastic clamshell holding a red velvet drawstring bag of cards.

who-am-i-33406.note: Retail hang-card backer with a wipe-clean pen attached.

ntama-2083.note: Ντάμα — solid marble draughts board with round pieces, no cardboard box.

connect-4-shots-258818.note: Molded blue plastic carrying case with a handle.

apagoreymeni-nisos-65244.note: Forbidden Island (Gamewright) ships in a rectangular METAL tin; art shows no rim.

sushi-go-133473.note: Sushi Go! ships in a small rectangular metal tin; art is flat. cornerR raised to match the tin's rounded corners in the art.

zombie-dice-horde-edition-224035.note: Ships in a cardboard tube; art is flat cover only, wraps the side.

cash-n-guns-second-edition-155362.note: Cash 'n' Guns 2nd ed ships in a metal tin with strongly rounded corners.
